TAKEN


IN BIG CASE

AFTER &c YEARS OF CON
TlNuoUs LITIGATION WILL
AGAIN GO TO THE HIGHER
COURT.

pages.raw_text:
In that famous and much It'll
gated case Of Joseph H. Boyd
against the Northern Pacific rail
road an appeal has been taken by
the railroad to the circuit court OF
appeals, sitting either at Seattle
or San Francisco, which court will
finally decide the matter.


The appeal is taken from the de-
cision of Judge Whitson of the fed.
eral court here, who awarded Boyd
the plaintiff, judgment against the
railroad for about $4,000.


This case Is one of the longest
in litigation in the memory of the
legal profession, the first COM
plant being filed in 1887, and has
been before the courts continuous.
ly ever since.


The first Judgment awarded
Boyd was for $4,000. One appeal
after another was taken to the va
rious courts on different legal
technicalities and the latest judg.
ment of Judge Whitson represents
the original amount of the claim
with accumulated interest after ~
years of litigation.


The case was originally predi-
Gated on the fact that D. c. Corbin,
at present owner of the Spokane
International, built narrow gauge
railroad to the Occur d'Alenes and
awarded to Boyd and others a con-
tract for part of the work. Boyd
alleged that he put 100000 into the
contract and that when 1t was fin
ished Corbin sold the road to the
Northern Pacific without paying
his claim.


1t was to recover this amount
that he brought the now famous
suit. 1t will be finally decided at
Seattle likely in September.
